title: Block Item
description: A wrapper item type — DISTINCT from `container` — that wraps exactly one inner content item and carries the cross-cutting, per-block concerns that apply uniformly to whatever it wraps: a required stable `id` (the patch/target anchor downstream tools address a block by), an optional `theme` override (whose value vocabulary is the theme tokens formalized in E2; opaque/pass-through here), a human `title`/`label`, and a `visibility` flag. Unlike `container`, a block groups NO grid and arranges nothing — it holds a single `content` leaf and applies its concerns to it. The wrapper-in-wrapper prohibition (a block never re-wraps a block) is enforced by the resolver grammar (E3-S2); this schema's single-content-child shape supports that rule. The surface is intentionally minimal and structured to be extended with further per-block concerns later. A block declares a configurable surface (its `title`/`label` and `visibility`) so those concerns are tunable through the existing configurable-surface mechanism.

constraints.description: The block's human heading/label — editable at runtime.
Whether the block (and the content it wraps) is shown — editable at runtime to toggle the block on or off.

id.title: Block Id
id.description: Stable, machine-readable identifier for this block, unique within the document. Required: it is the anchor a patch or a configurator target addresses this block by. Distinct from an instance's optional layout-local `id`; the block's identity is part of its config so it survives independent of placement.

title.title: Block Title
title.description: Optional human-readable heading/label for the block, rendered with the wrapped content. Tunable at runtime via the configurable surface.

theme.title: Block Theme Override
theme.description: Optional per-block theme override. Its value is drawn from the theme vocabulary formalized in E2; here the field is present and passed through opaquely — validation of the value against the theme vocab lands in E2-S3. Omit to inherit the surrounding theme.

visibility.title: Block Visibility
visibility.description: Whether the block is shown. Defaults to true (visible). Tunable at runtime via the configurable surface to hide or reveal the block and the content it wraps.

content.title: Block Content
content.description: The single inner content item this block wraps. A block permits EXACTLY ONE content child — one item instance referencing an item-type schema via $ref. The block applies its per-block concerns (theme, visibility, title) to this one content leaf. The resolver enforces the wrapper grammar (no wrapper-in-wrapper) in E3-S2.
